issue_title: The api client should send connection upgrade headers
issue_body:
To hint proxies about connection hijacking, docker clients should send connection upgrade headers like the docker cli does.

From https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/api/docker_remote_api_v1.24/#/4-2-hijacking:

> In this version of the API, /attach, uses hijacking to transport stdin, stdout, and stderr on the same socket.
>
> To hint potential proxies about connection hijacking, Docker client sends connection upgrade headers similarly to websocket.

```
Upgrade: tcp
Connection: Upgrade
```

On Docker for Desktop, the proxy that sits between Docker Compose and the daemon will not be aware that the connection will be hijacked. This can lead to an issue where the proxy will install a CloseNotifier and just after that will hijack the connection, which is know to be incompatible. See https://github.com/docker/compose/issues/3685

See also https://github.com/docker/compose/issues/3700

issue_title: docker-py v2.0.0  Can't create container data volumes using client.containers.run & create
issue_body:
[https://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/containers.html](url): **run** method: for **volumes** it says:
```
volumes (dict or list) –

A dictionary to configure volumes mounted inside the container. The key is either the host path or a volume name, and the value is a dictionary with the keys:

    bind The path to mount the volume inside the container
    mode Either rw to mount the volume read/write, or ro to mount it read-only.

For example:

{'/home/user1/': {'bind': '/mnt/vol2', 'mode': 'rw'},
 '/var/www': {'bind': '/mnt/vol1', 'mode': 'ro'}}
```

This effectively means that you can't create container data volumes, since you either have to create a volume 1st using **docker create volume** or use a host directory.
There is no option to just specify a container folder.

The  low-level API ([https://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#module-docker.api.container](url)) **create_container** still allows you to do this (since I was able to do this using the v1 of docker-py

From the **docker run** documentation [https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/run/](url):
```
  -v, --volume value                Bind mount a volume (default []). The format
                                    is `[host-src:]container-dest[:<options>]`.
                                    The comma-delimited `options` are [rw|ro],
                                    [z|Z], [[r]shared|[r]slave|[r]private], and
                                    [nocopy]. The 'host-src' is an absolute path
                                    or a name value.
```

Am I correct here that logic to support container data volumes is missing from **client.containers.run & create**?
Or is there another way how to do this? Or are container data volumes being replaced by data volumes?

It's my impression that, as of version 3.0, `get_archive` now returns an iterator over the archive's content rather than as a file-like object.

Is this correct? I so, I'd be happy to submit a quick PR to the documentation noting this change.

issue_body: I think it would be useful if the orientation of the video frames and the trajectory plots was the same.  At the moment on the walkthrough code that I have been using the plots can be confusing due to the flipping over of the trajectory plot wrt the video frame.  Also the trajectory plot is x and y trimmed to fit the tracked particles rather than left with the same dimensions as the video file.  This is especially noticeable when the video frame has a few particles near the middle of the frame.  In this case the two representations are barely recognisable, in my case at least.

Clicking the walkthrough link leads to an almost blank page:

http://soft-matter.github.io/trackpy/dev/tutorial/walkthrough.html

Hello!

As mentioned in #15, I'd like to propose a solution for handling elasticsearch-dsl's `Object` fields (or ES objects in general). During the flattening of nested objects in Elasticsearch, fields in form of:

```
    country = Object(properties={
        "id": Integer(),
        "name": Text()
    })
```
are transformed into a flat structure, as can be seen here: [link](https://db.tt/ibHuGPIEfQ)

Due to the fact, that here: https://github.com/jurismarches/luqum/blob/a8002c293ebc5d90ff879fa36e9282ea71eca2a8/luqum/elasticsearch/visitor.py#L211-L213

is the `'.' in node.name` check present, parsed queries in form of `country.name:Italy` will be transformed to a nested query, which will cause hiccups in Elasticsearch.

Now, the `country.name` seems to be parsed correctly by YACC as dot isn't a special char. It's the check in the mentioned file causes the problem for me here.

What have I done - I've subclassed ElasticsearchQueryBuilder as follows:

```
class ElasticsearchDotAwareQueryBuilder(ElasticsearchQueryBuilder):
    def _is_nested(self, node):
        for child in node.children:
            if isinstance(child, SearchField):
                return True
            elif self._is_nested(child):
                return True

        return False
```

And now I have both `:` available for nested fields, as well as `.` for flat objects.

I didn't have enough time to dive deeply but I think it shouldn't break other functionalities. My question is - why was the check for dot presence there in the first place - is it just an alternative to `:`, just not introduced on parsing level but later in the query building process, or am I missing something?

issue_title: AWS termination policy semi-broke cluster after instance going down
issue_body:
At one of our deis clusters one of instances went down (it failed health checks). Autoscaling group took 'arbitrary' decision to terminate it and boot up new one. In result we've two initial deis instances (part of etcd cluster) and one new (just an etcd proxy), our etcd cluster isn't healthy, we could fix it by updating user data and tinkering with etcd api to replace node and reconfigure new node as part of cluster.

Best case scenario would be to have time to reboot instance (it probably failed to some issues with underlying hardware and reboot would help) and if it doesn't work then we can try to replace instance.

So the issue is, default AWS default Deis deployment scripts configuration is a little bit 'broken' or at least not suitable for production usage.

Are there any known best practices to avoid such situations?

issue_title: Out of disk space on machine running deis-builder
issue_body:
1 machine from the cluster (the one where `deis-builder` is running) runs out of space very quickly. 

We've figured out that it's `deis-builder-data` that dramatically eats space. And it grows fast with each deploy because `deis-builder` doesn't seem to fully clean the intermediate containers. 

The solution right now is to stop `deis-builder`, remove `deis-builder-data` container **with** its volume (`-v` option) and run `deis-builder` again:
```bash
# Log into the machine that is running deis-builder and:
deisctl stop builder
docker rm -v deis-builder-data
deisctl start builder
```

ref #1678

issue_body: see discussion in #2044

https://github.com/deis/deis/blame/master/docs/understanding_deis/components.rst#L39

I thought we'd fixed this a while ago, but I guess not.  It's not quite accurate.  Configs are not actually ever baked into the images.

```
→deis releases      
=== stagingapi Releases
v11     2017-01-04T10:44:04Z    f deployed 93924ad
v10     2017-01-04T10:22:24Z    f deployed f6e80f8
v9      2017-01-04T10:06:22Z    f deployed 3e367ee
```

It would be easier to read logs, if local time is used instead of UTC.

When using a stateless platform the graceful shutdown script can't be used as it assumes you are running ceph and the data store tooling.

The very last part of the graceful shutdown is still required by a stateless platform.  The nodes must be removed from ETCD.

On a similar note I don't see the graceful shutdown script removing the node from the ETCD Discovery URL.

Need to investigate

issue_title: Add a transaction by hash lookup
issue_body:
Need to support a transaction lookup by hash for the json-rpc method `eth_getTransactionByHash`.

First impression for an approach:
- add transactions to the key-value store (even pending transactions should be retrievable)
- key = hash, value = transaction root & index (so that we can reuse logic/data in `chaindb.get_block_transactions` and don't store the whole transaction twice in the db)
- need to add transactions in these scenarios
  - import block
  - create transaction locally
  - ?

Alternatives:
- Store the transaction body once with a key of the transaction hash, and store the transaction hash under the key= transaction root & index (the reverse of the suggestion above, since transaction lookup by hash might be more common and the cost of a double lookup might be high)

Open questions
- some kind of garbage cleanup for pending transactions that never get included in a block

issue_title: Add a test suite for migrations
issue_body:
Seems like proper behavior when dealing with migrations have been troubling you. Maybe it'd be useful adding a test suite around proper migration output for south/1.7?

I could pitch in later this week if you think a migration test suite belongs to the project (I think it does).

Given an MRS for sentence 'Nonetheless, it is worth watching.' produced by ACE:
```
[ LTOP: h0
INDEX: e2 [ e SF: prop TENSE: pres MOOD: indicative PROG: - PERF: - ]
RELS: < [ "_nonetheless_a_1_rel"<0:12> LBL: h1 ARG0: e4 [ e SF: prop TENSE: untensed MOOD: indicative PROG: - PERF: - ] ARG1: h5 ]
 [ pron_rel<13:15> LBL: h6 ARG0: x3 [ x PERS: 3 NUM: sg GEND: n PRONTYPE: std_pron ] ]
 [ pronoun_q_rel<13:15> LBL: h7 ARG0: x3 RSTR: h8 BODY: h9 ]
 [ "_worth_a_1_rel"<19:24> LBL: h10 ARG0: i11 ARG1: h12 ]
 [ "_watch_v_1_rel"<25:34> LBL: h10 ARG0: e13 [ e SF: prop-or-ques TENSE: untensed MOOD: indicative PROG: + PERF: - ] ARG1: i14 ARG2: x3 ] >
HCONS: < h0 qeq h1 h5 qeq h10 h8 qeq h6 h12 qeq h10 > ]
```
conversion to DMRS using pyDelphin does not produce the correct result:
```
<dmrs cfrom="-1" cto="-1">
<node cfrom="0" cto="12" nodeid="10000"><realpred lemma="nonetheless" pos="a" sense="1" /><sortinfo cvarsort="e" mood="indicative" perf="-" prog="-" sf="prop" tense="untensed" /></node>
<node cfrom="13" cto="15" nodeid="10001"><gpred>pron_rel</gpred><sortinfo cvarsort="x" gend="n" num="sg" pers="3" prontype="std_pron" /></node>
<node cfrom="13" cto="15" nodeid="10002"><gpred>pronoun_q_rel</gpred><sortinfo /></node>
<node cfrom="19" cto="24" nodeid="10003"><realpred lemma="worth" pos="a" sense="1" /><sortinfo cvarsort="i" /></node>
<node cfrom="25" cto="34" nodeid="10004"><realpred lemma="watch" pos="v" sense="1" /><sortinfo cvarsort="e" mood="indicative" perf="-" prog="+" sf="prop-or-ques" tense="untensed" /></node>
<link from="0" to="10000"><rargname /><post>H</post></link>
<link from="10000" to="10003"><rargname>ARG1</rargname><post>H</post></link>
<link from="10002" to="10001"><rargname>RSTR</rargname><post>H</post></link>
<link from="10003" to="10003"><rargname>ARG1</rargname><post>H</post></link>
<link from="10003" to="10004"><rargname /><post>EQ</post></link>
<link from="10004" to="10001"><rargname>ARG2</rargname><post>NEQ</post></link>
</dmrs>
```
Note the link ARG1 from 10003 to 10003, and EQ link from 10003 to 10004. 

In contrast, a version of pyDelphin forked on Nov 4, 2014 does not encounter this problem, producing correct DMRS:

```
<dmrs cfrom="-1" cto="-1">
<node cfrom="0" cto="12" nodeid="10000"><realpred lemma="nonetheless" pos="a" sense="1" /><sortinfo cvarsort="e" mood="indicative" perf="-" prog="-" sf="prop" tense="untensed" /></node>
<node cfrom="13" cto="15" nodeid="10001"><gpred>pronoun_q_rel</gpred><sortinfo /></node>
<node cfrom="13" cto="15" nodeid="10002"><gpred>pron_rel</gpred><sortinfo cvarsort="x" gend="n" num="sg" pers="3" prontype="std_pron" /></node>
<node cfrom="19" cto="24" nodeid="10003"><realpred lemma="worth" pos="a" sense="1" /><sortinfo cvarsort="i" /></node>
<node cfrom="25" cto="34" nodeid="10004"><realpred lemma="watch" pos="v" sense="1" /><sortinfo cvarsort="e" mood="indicative" perf="-" prog="+" sf="prop-or-ques" tense="untensed" /></node>
<link from="0" to="10000"><rargname /><post>H</post></link>
<link from="10000" to="10004"><rargname>ARG1</rargname><post>H</post></link>
<link from="10001" to="10002"><rargname>RSTR</rargname><post>H</post></link>
<link from="10003" to="10004"><rargname>ARG1</rargname><post>H</post></link>
<link from="10004" to="10002"><rargname>ARG2</rargname><post>NEQ</post></link>
</dmrs>
```

The conversion is done by loading the MRS string as simpleMRS and then dumping it as dmrx. You can see full code here if needed: https://github.com/matichorvat/pydmrs/blob/master/mrs_to_dmrs/mrs_to_dmrs.py

TypedFeatureStructures implement __getitem__ access for keys like `t['SYNSEM.LOCAL']` or `t['SYNSEM']['LOCAL']`, so it should also provide __contains__ to check if such a key exists. That is, one could say `'SYNSEM' in t` or `'SYNSEM.LOCAL' in t` or `'LOCAL' in t['SYNSEM']`.

issue_title: convert command needs custom data specifiers for selecting from profiles
issue_body:
The `delphin convert` command can take a profile path as an argument, in which case MRSs are pulled from the `result:mrs` field for conversion. The table/column is hard-coded, and thus don't work for non-standard schemata (e.g. from XMT).

Provide a `--select=DATASPEC` parameter, defaulting to `result:mrs`, to help with this.

ENV PATH /opt/conda/bin:$PATH

issue_title: On silent fail of update, indicate which package(s) are preventing an update
issue_body:
## Current Behavior I want to update dask. I try with `conda update dask`, but I'm told it is already installed. I know it is already installed, but I want to update it to the latest version, which I don't currently have. I believe this is happening because I have `anaconda=5.2.0` in my current environment spec. To the typical user I imagine that doing `conda update foo` and having nothing happen would indicate to them that they already have the latest version (esp. given there is no information to the contrary). ### Steps to Reproduce ``` $ conda update dask dask-core Solving environment: done ==> WARNING: A newer version of conda exists. <== current version: 4.5.8 latest version: 4.5.11 Please update conda by running $ conda update -n base -c defaults conda # All requested packages already installed. ``` ## Expected Behavior I believe conda internally is seeing that there is a newer version of dask but that it can't update to this because `anaconda=5.2.0` is in the current environment spec, and pins the dask version to the older currently installed version. I would like conda to let me in on this insight, rather than show me the exact same output I'd get if I actually did have the latest version already installed. I can get the "update" by asking to install the exact latest version: ``` $ conda install dask=0.18.2 Solving environment: done ==> WARNING: A newer version of conda exists. <== current version: 4.5.8 latest version: 4.5.11 Please update conda by running $ conda update -n base -c defaults conda ## Package Plan ## environment location: /Users/ijstokes/anaconda3/envs/dasktut added / updated specs: - dask=0.18.2 The following packages will be downloaded: package | build ---------------------------|----------------- certifi-2018.8.24 | py36_1 139 KB The following packages will be REMOVED: anaconda: 5.2.0-py36_3 The following packages will be UPDATED: certifi: 2018.4.16-py36_0 --> 2018.8.24-py36_1 dask: 0.17.5-py36_0 --> 0.18.2-py36_0 dask-core: 0.17.5-py36_0 --> 0.18.2-py36_0 distributed: 1.21.8-py36_0 --> 1.22.1-py36_0 openssl: 1.0.2o-h26aff7b_0 --> 1.0.2p-h1de35cc_0 ``` ## Environment Information
`conda info`

``` $ conda info active environment : base active env location : /Users/ijstokes/anaconda3 shell level : 1 user config file : /Users/ijstokes/.condarc populated config files : /Users/ijstokes/.condarc conda version : 4.5.8 conda-build version : 3.10.5 python version : 3.6.5.final.0 base environment : /Users/ijstokes/anaconda3 (writable) channel URLs : https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main/osx-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/main/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/free/osx-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/free/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r/osx-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/r/noarch https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/pro/osx-64 https://repo.anaconda.com/pkgs/pro/noarch package cache : /Users/ijstokes/anaconda3/pkgs /Users/ijstokes/.conda/pkgs envs directories : /Users/ijstokes/anaconda3/envs /Users/ijstokes/.conda/envs platform : osx-64 user-agent : conda/4.5.8 requests/2.18.4 CPython/3.6.5 Darwin/17.7.0 OSX/10.13.6 UID:GID : 2553821:345243045 netrc file : None offline mode : False ```

issue_title: activate should do a check to verify env hasn't been relocated
issue_body: Most users won't know the details of how `conda activate foo` works, and could reasonably assume that `~/anaconda/envs/foo` could be safely relocated to `~/anaconda/envs/bar` using `mv`.  This will not work, and attempts to `source activate bar` will result in error messages and an obliterated `PATH` (see bug #354).

issue_body:

      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/main.py", line 145, in _main
        exit_code = args.func(args, p)
      File "../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/main_create.py", line 68, in execute
        install(args, parser, 'create')
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/cli/install.py", line 191, in install
        explicit(specs, prefix, verbose=not context.quiet, index_args=index_args)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/misc.py", line 187, in explicit
        execute_actions(actions, index=index, verbose=verbose)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/plan.py", line 643, in execute_actions
        inst.execute_instructions(plan, index, verbose)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/instructions.py", line 134, in execute_instructions
        cmd(state, arg)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/instructions.py", line 58, in EXTRACT_CMD
        extract(arg)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/install.py", line 807, in extract
        exp_backoff_fn(os.rename, temp_path, path)
      File ".../miniconda3/lib/python3.5/site-packages/conda/common/disk.py", line 148, in exp_backoff_fn
        return fn(*args, **kwargs)
    FileExistsError: [Errno 17] File exists: '.../miniconda3/pkgs/zlib-1.2.8-3.tmp' -> '.../miniconda3/pkgs/zlib-1.2.8-3'

```

The main issue AFAICT is that some process has a file under the pkgs/zlib-1.2.8-3 folder open (probably a .so). When install.extract() tries to rm_rf() the target package path, NFS v3 uses "silly rename" to act like the open file got deleted, but it leaves a .nfs....... file in place of the original file. So the rm_rf of the target package path silently fails in install.extract(), then the os.rename loudly fails.

The .nfs...... files will not be removed until the process that has them open exits. In the case of a daemon process, this could be a while.

A "silly rename" like work-around is:
```
--- install.py	2017-01-18 14:26:31.394302133 +0000
+++ install.py.new	2017-01-18 15:23:46.090889706 +0000
@@ -35,6 +35,7 @@
 import sys
 import tarfile
 import traceback
+import datetime
 from collections import namedtuple
 from enum import Enum
 from itertools import chain
@@ -805,6 +806,12 @@
         with tarfile.open(fname) as t:
             t.extractall(path=temp_path)
         rm_rf(path)
+        # In case the rm_rf does not fully remove everything (e.g. permission
+        # denied, open files on NFS mount, etc.), move it out of the way
+        if os.path.exists(path):
+            timestamp = datetime.datetime.now().isoformat().replace(':', '_')
+            backup_path = path + '.' + timestamp
+            exp_backoff_fn(os.rename, path, backup_path)
         exp_backoff_fn(os.rename, temp_path, path)
         if sys.platform.startswith('linux') and os.getuid() == 0:
             # When extracting as root, tarfile will by restore ownership
```

But this will likely require manual cleanup.

I don't know if this has come up before but I see this as a low hanging fruit and an immensely useful feature.

Platform detection is currently done like so in `config.py`:

``` python
_sys_map = {'linux2': 'linux', 'linux': 'linux',
            'darwin': 'osx', 'win32': 'win'}
platform = _sys_map.get(sys.platform, 'unknown')
bits = 8 * tuple.__itemsize__

if platform == 'linux' and machine() == 'armv6l':
    subdir = 'linux-armv6l'
    arch_name = 'armv6l'
else:
    subdir = '%s-%d' % (platform, bits)
    arch_name = {64: 'x86_64', 32: 'x86'}[bits]
```

Would it be possible to allow user to add custom platforms?

You can potentially have multiple "linux" builds of a package which is quite common in the unix world (e.g. we have a use case where we build Cython extensions that are not portable between Linux platforms due to mismatching glibc versions etc -- so we have to serve multiple "linux-64" channels). It would be nice if conda could be pointed to look at user-defined platform subdirs (sort of like it does with noarch) like

```
<url>/my-platform-1
<url>/my-platform-2
```

(or combine the default platform with that to have `my-platform-1/linux-64` or `/linux-64/my-platform-1`).

One way of doing this, I guess, would be to allow for `CONDA_PLATFORM` environment variable -- if not set, the current default platform detection method is used; if set, the urls looked up will be

``` sh
<url>/$CONDA_PLATFORM/
<url>/noarch/
```

issue_title: Allow "channel" in command line for conda info

Most of the conda commands allow specification of a channel at the command line with the -c or --channel parameters. Conda info does not, which is inconsistent and makes it more difficult to work with multiple channels and/or labels.

issue_title: conda env processing to provide more helpful messages
issue_body:
From the Anaconda ML (see last half of [this thread](https://groups.google.com/a/continuum.io/forum/#!topic/anaconda/oNZzhJKwA9g) ) :

> I have my yml file at place the file looks like this:
> ls ps-env shows the file

```yaml
name: ps-env
dependecies:
  - python = 3.5.2
  - pandas = 0.17.1
  - numpy = 1.13.1
  - sklearn = 0.19.0
  - scipy = 0.19.1
  - tensorflow
  - keras
  - imbalanced-learn
```

> I still get the error. It doesnt make sense to me

Can you spot the problem?  I tried myself, putting that YAML into a file `testml.yaml`, and got:

```
$ conda env create --file testml.yaml 
Using Anaconda API: https://api.anaconda.org

SpecNotFound: Can't process without a name
```

Someone on the ML pointed out the issue: spelling mistake in the YAML.  I'd suggest a few things:

* improve the error message to say something like:

    ```
    "dependencies" section not found, unable to create conda environment
    ```

* report any sections that are found that are unexpected and are going to be ignored (such as `dependecies`

* use something like Kwalify (and perhaps [pykwalify](https://github.com/Grokzen/pykwalify) ) to test/assert an open schema on the JSON for env files

When happening data to a ctable, a type casting might be necessary. Consider the following example:

```python 
bc = bcolz.ctable([bcolz.carray([1,2,3]), bcolz.carray(['a', 'b', 'c'])], names=['a', 'b'] )
bc2 = bcolz.ctable([bcolz.carray([1,2,3]), bcolz.carray(['dd', 'ee', 'ff'])], names=['a', 'b'] )
bc.append(bc2)
bc
```

This outputs:
```python 
ctable((6,), [('a', '<i4'), ('b', '<U1')])
  nbytes: 48; cbytes: 32.00 KB; ratio: 0.00
  cparams := cparams(clevel=5, shuffle=True, cname='blosclz')
[(1, 'a') (2, 'b') (3, 'c') (1, 'd') (2, 'e') (3, 'f')]
```

In this case, column b of bc2 is truncated to 'U1' while one might want to change that type to 'U2'.

Would it make sense to allow recasting a particular column (or set of columns), along the lines of:
```python 
bc = bcolz.ctable([bcolz.carray([1,2,3]), bcolz.carray(['a', 'b', 'c'])], names=['a', 'b'] )
bc2 = bcolz.ctable([bcolz.carray([1,2,3]), bcolz.carray(['dd', 'ee', 'ff'])], names=['a', 'b'] )
bc.cast_col('b', 'U2') # hypothetical method
bc.append(bc2)
bc
``` 

that would lead to the output:
```python 
ctable((6,), [('a', '<i4'), ('b', '<U2')])
  nbytes: 48; cbytes: 32.00 KB; ratio: 0.00
  cparams := cparams(clevel=5, shuffle=True, cname='blosclz')
[(1, 'a') (2, 'b') (3, 'c') (1, 'dd') (2, 'ee') (3, 'ff')]
```

so that the append call does not truncate the appended data?

Is there a better way to approach this issue of appending data that does not fit in the existing columns of the ctable?

issue_title: az acs create should support associating a registry with a cluster
issue_body:
To assist in the initial configurations, az acr create could add a registry parameter that would associate a container registry with the cluster. The service principal would be registered with ACR with readonly permissions for docker pull. 
I'm not suggesting az acs create should create a registry as we expect customers to use registries across multiple services. In the Azure Portal we might optimize the creation of new, or link existing experiences. The additional parameters needed on az acs create to create a new registry feels more complicated than running that command prior then linking as a secondary command. 
```
--container-registry -r               : Associates a Service Principal between the cluster and the registry, providing docker pull capabilities. 
```

**Blocking Issues:**

- The tests cannot be re-recorded currently as some pre-req resources are required that aren't obvious to create.
Service Fabric team to take a look at this.
See https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/4328

issue_title: Installer fails due to urllib problem
issue_body:
Urllib sends malformed gets through our proxy that simply won't work with a proxy that scrutinizes TLS connections.  From what I gather urllib has a long torrid history w/ HTTPS and perhaps something like Requests should be used instead.  

Here's where the problems start:
https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/blob/master/scripts/curl_install_pypi/install.py#L121

`2017-03-28 19:27:53 my.ip.address - - invalid_request DENIED "Technology/Internet" - 400 TCP_NC_MISS GET - https pypi.python.org 443 /packages/source/v/virtualenv/virtualenv-15.0.0.tar.gz - gz "Python-urllib/1.17" 151.101.32.223 867 126 -`

Command signatures
```
az appservice web config connection-string update -g myRG -n myWeb --type MySQL --connections conn1=<value1>;conn2=<value2> --slot-connections conn3=<value1>

az appservice web config connection-string delete -g myRG -n myWeb --connection conn1

az appservice web config connection-string show -g myRG -n myWeb
```

issue_title: Is there a metadata generation tool like `azure --gen` in this version?
issue_body:
In old versions of the Azure CLI, there was a flag `azure --gen` which generated JSON metadata with info about all the commands. This was really helpful for creating tab completion extensions (see e.g. https://github.com/stuartleeks/posh-azurecli).

I want to make an attempt at porting Posh-AzureCli to the the 2.x version of the CLI, but it relies on the metadata generated with `azure --gen` and I can't figure out how to extract the corresponding metadata from `az`.

Is metadata export available at all today? If not, would you consider adding it?

1. Ensure the default config is right. The current version has visible defects
2. Configured metrics can load in portal

issue_title: -timeout parameter for az storage blob download
issue_body:
For an advisory support, case, I'm looking for the default value and/or min/max values of the -timeout parameter on `az storage blob download`.
I tried logging that [here](https://github.com/Azure/azure-docs-cli-python/issues/968#issuecomment-409670831) and was directed to this repo.
The info in the reference is the same as what I found with the -h parameter, only stating "Request timeout in seconds. Applies to each call to the service."

Can you provide any more details? Thanks!

The ~/.azure/accessTokens.json file that is generated as a result of az login (and read by e.g. the terraform module) contains refresh_token sections. To my understanding this means that when the access_tokens have expired, it should be possible to use the refresh_tokens to get new access tokens and avoid the need to actually log in again (which is a bit cumbersome for the manual login step that requires a browser visit).

My suggestion therefore is to add a flag "-refresh" to login and implement this.

issue_title: VaultSecretGroup: TypeError: 'Subresource' object is not iterable   [jython]
issue_body:
When using Azure SDK under jython, in case if we configure VaultSecretGroup - we get unexpected exception  

TypeError: 'Subresource' object is not iterable 

This is caused by fact, that in 
https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-mgmt-compute/azure/mgmt/compute/v2017_03_30/models/vault_secret_group.py#L28

we have mapping
```
'source_vault': {'key': 'sourceVault', 'type': 'SubResource'},
```

while right mapping which plays well, seems to be 

```
'source_vault': {'key': 'sourceVault', 'type': 'SourceVault'},
```

i.e. portion of the code might be

```
...
VaultSecretGroup(
                source_vault=SourceVault(id=vault_info.id),
                vault_certificates=[
                    VaultCertificate(
                        certificate_url=certificate_url,
                        certificate_store="My"
                    )
                ]
            )
...
```

The change also does not harm original python flow.

Hello, Andrew. Thanks for awesome framework.
Small question: how to render templates for HTTP errors using Jinja2?

For example:

```python
class Pages(web.View):
    """ Pages class """

    @aiohttp_jinja2.template('pages.jinja2')
    async def get(self):
        # Simplify request.match_info[...]
        request = self.request.match_info

        # Founded slug
        if request['slug'] == 'about':
            response = {
                'title': request['slug'],
                'body': {
                    'heading': request['slug'],
                },
            }
            # Return response data
            return response

issue_title: Cannot send multipart response from aiohttp server with unbounded range for streaming
issue_body:
## tl;dr

StreamResponse seems unable to send data without being chunked.  

## My specific situation

My application is writing and appending mp4 video data to disk and needs serve this video file as it is being written and continue until a timeout is reached and EOF is reached.  A normal Response object will not do, due to the requirement of having a fully complete response body.  StreamResponse would *seem* to be the logical response class.

However, the StreamResponse sets the Transfer-Encoding response header to chunked, even though the StreamResponse.chunked variable returns False. 

## Expected behavior

If chunked options is false, and perhaps the request being prepared includes a Range request chunked encoding should be turned off.  Another option include having a MultipartWriter sub-class in the response that allows for the response to be written in true streaming fashion with boundary handling.  Issuing these requests in a context would make sense.

## Actual behaviour

StreamResponse seemingly always sets Transfer-Encoding to chunked.

## Environment

AioHTTP Version: 3.1.3
Python Version: 3.6.3
Arch: ARMv7
Virtual ENV
And mention it in client documentation.

issue_title: nbgrader release feedback (and show in assignment list)
issue_body:
Hi,

I think nbgrader should have a command like nbgrader release feedback which transfers the feedback to some exchange service, where students can then fetch it using the assignment list, similar to how they fetch assignments.

I am currently using a python script which generates an ipynb with links to the html feedback files and then (as root) transfers the feedback into a folder in each students home directory.

However this is confusing for students, since they expect to use the assignment list for viewing their feedback.

After looking through formgrade with the goal of updating it to be a JupyterHub 0.7.0 service. I have been trying to think through ways of simplifying formgrade. Some thoughts:

* Remove the ability of formgrade users to access live single user notebook servers. @minrk is working on an example of running a single user notebook server as a JupyterHub service with group level access (https://github.com/jupyterhub/jupyterhub/issues/921).
* This change would make it *much* easier to migrate formgrade to being a pretty stock JupyterHub service.
* I am also starting to explore replacing formgrade entirely by a live notebook server/jupyterlab with additional nbextensions/labextensions. Some initial ideas below...

The core client UI of formgrade could be replace in many situation by a live notebook with the following:

* An nbextension that allow human grading and commenting and stores the results in nbgrader metadata.
* A new nbgrader command line phase that takes that metadata and stores it in the db. We already have nbconvert preprocessors that essentially do this, so this would be super simple.
* Some sort of additional frontend UI that makes it easy to navigate through notebooks for grading.

In JupyterLab, offering additional UIs is relatively easy. I don't have a ton of time right now to work on this stuff, but I may pick away at it.

issue_title: Gradient of norm doesn't account for axis argument
issue_body: I see that Autograd currently implements the gradient of norm in numpy.linalg, but it doesn't handle any of its options. Support for the "axis" option would be useful.

Dear Autograd,
Nice tool! Learning it right now.

I am calculating the hessian of a scalar valued function. Given a function, f, and a vector input x of length n, hessian(f)(x) returns an array of shape (n, n, n). For example:
```python
    import autograd.numpy as np
    from autograd import hessian

    def f(x):
        y = np.exp(x)
        return y

    xx = np.ones(4)
    ddf = hessian(f)
    print(ddf(xx).shape)
    # (4,4,4)
```

This is not the definition of the hessian matrix as given by [wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hessian_matrix). Instead, obtaining the relevant matrix requires doing the following operation:
```python
    print(ddf(xx).diagonal(axis1=1, axis2=2))
```

May I suggest updating what hessian returns to correspond with the definition in wikipedia?

Thank you!

issue_title: Implement as PEP 516 build tool, providing PEP 517 build backend interface, configured with pypackage.toml and CMakeLists.txt
issue_body:
With the end goal of being a build system tool for [PEP 518](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0518/) with good C extension support, scikit-build should be implemented as a [PEP 516](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0516/) build tool providing the build backend interface specified in [PEP 517](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0517/).

Following the vision specified at the end of [this SciPy talk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oE5iePv8nD8), most of the packaging specification should be provided declaratively in a `pypackage.toml` in the `[tool.skbuild]` section. Additional, more complicated packaging information, like how to build the C extensions, is provided in the CMake `CMakeLists.txt` configuration.

This means, like [flit](http://flit.readthedocs.io/), we generate wheels directly by creating [the wheel layout specified in PEP 427](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0427/). These wheels can be generated by calling an `skbuild` script followed by the subcommands specified in [PEP 516](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0516/) or by the API specified in [PEP 517](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0517/). We will [create a shim (that can also be placed in `setup.py` )](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0516/#setuptools-shim) when `python setup.py install` is called that will install the generated wheel so skbuild functionality can be used immediately with `pip`, a manual call to `python setup.py install`, or `conda`. But, if we follow PEP 516, 517, 518, when these PEPs are finalized and implemented in pip and conda, the setup.py shim will no longer be needed.

Steps forward:

- [ ] 1. **Implement `skbuild metadata` subcommand**. This is the `metadata` subcommand specified in PEP 516. It outputs the `METADATA` file contents (see PEP 427) to stdout. These are the metadata keys described in [PEP 345](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0345/). A package that uses scikit-build should specify these in a `pypackage.toml` file in the `[tool.skbuild]` section, probably with [lower-case key variants, a la flit](http://flit.readthedocs.io/en/latest/flit_ini.html#scripts-section). We can use [`wheel.pkginfo`]( http://wheel.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html#module-wheel.pkginfo) to help with this and [pytoml](https://github.com/avakar/pytoml)

- [ ] 2. **Implement `skbuild.api.get_wheel_metadata`**. This implements the `get_wheel_metadata` method as specified in PEP-517, which creates the wheel `{package}-{version}.distinfo` directory in the specified location and populates it with the `METADATA` file from Step 1. along with the `WHEEL` file (see PEP 427).

- [ ] 3. **Implement the `skbuild wheel` subcommand**. This is the PEP 516 `wheel` subcommand. Internally, it should call `skbuild.api.build_wheel` as specified in PEP 517. This can be created with the assistance of `wheel.archive` subcommand. Our `[tool.skbuild]` section of pypackage.toml can have additional keys, `py_modules`, a list of Pure Python modules to install directly into the wheel,  `py_packages`, a list of Pure Python packages to install directly into the wheel, and `data`, directories to install directly into the `.data/data` section of the wheel. Also, a `scripts` with a list of *script, entrypoint tuples*. Then *cmake* should be configured with `CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX` set to the wheel root directory. Other CMake variables to specify during configuration: `WHEEL_PURELIB`, which is set to `{package}-{version}.data/purelib`,  and similar for `WHEEL_PLATLIB`, `WHEEL_HEADERS`, and `WHEEL_DATA`. Finally, there should a section in pypackage.toml that specifies what CMake `install` command `COMPONENTS` should be installed. 

- [ ] 4. **Implement the `skbuild install` subcommand**. This can just delegate to `wheel.install` as a convenient way to install a package. Check out `flit install`.

- [ ] 5. **Implement the setup.py shim**. This can effectively call `skbuild install` .

- [ ] 4. **Implement the `skbuild build_requires` subcommand**. This is the PEP 516 `build_requires` subcommand, which should call `skbuild.api.get_build_requires` as specified in PEP 517. This could output the content of a pypackage.toml metadata entry along with other requirements like a *cmake* python package that encapsulates CMake, etc.

- [ ] 4. **Implement the `skbuild develop` subcommand**. This is the PEP 516 `develop` subcommand. Just error out; I don't think it is reasonable to support this.

The current approach of wrapping setuptools does not get us closer to PEP 516/517/518 support. The monkeypatching of setuptools, which is monkeypatching distutils, and trying to masquerade C-extension as package_data is problematic (see e.g. #117, #106, #84, #107). It does not move towards better pypackage.toml adoption and independence from distutils/setuptools. It should be abandoned.

This issue centralizes all tasks related to documentation improvements

* [ ] Update [Examples for scikit-build developers](http://scikit-build.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html#examples-for-scikit-build-developers)

* Cross-compilation:

  * [ ] Update [Introduction to dockross](http://scikit-build.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html#introduction-to-dockross)
  * [ ] Update [Using dockcross-manylinux to generate Linux wheels ](http://scikit-build.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html#using-dockcross-manylinux-to-generate-linux-wheels)
  * [ ] Update [Using dockcross-mingwpy to generate Windows wheels](http://scikit-build.readthedocs.io/en/latest/usage.html#using-dockcross-mingwpy-to-generate-windows-wheels)

issue_title: Separate naming, storage, and profile APIs
issue_body:
@shea256, @muneeb-ali, @larrysalibra, and myself talked this over, and we feel that in order to support lite clients (e.g. mobile), it will be necessary to move most of the Core functionality off of the device.  Moreover, the functionality may be split across multiple endpoints.  For example, a mobile client may use a 3rd party service's naming API endpoint for doing name management, but may use a separate, personal storage API endpoint (e.g. running in something like Google AppEngine or Amazon Lambda) for loading and storing data to/from their storage providers.

In order to support this, we need to tweak Blockstack Core so that the storage and naming APIs can run in separate instances.  In particular:

* The Core node only indexes the blockchain, participates in the Atlas network, and queues up transactions for sending if its naming API endpoint is activated.  Otherwise, the node does not do anything in the background (making it suitable for running in PaaS platforms).
* The Core node only loads and runs storage drivers if its storage API endpoint is activated.  Otherwise, it never tries to connect to remote storage services nor needs to import storage-specific modules.
* The profiles APIs shouldn't be in the Core server at all.  Only clients should be responsible for parsing a name's zone file and decoding the profile.

issue_title: pysc2.bin.play: mod or map dependencies...no longer available
issue_body:
I am able to run replays from the replay pack just fine using:

`python -m pysc2.bin.play --replay <path/to/replay>`

But when I try to run the same command on a personal replay from a 1v1 ladder match, I get the following error: 

`SC2APIProtocol.ResponseStartReplay.Error.LaunchError: 'The  launch of this game makes reference to mod or map dependencies which are no longer available.'`

Full console dump [here](https://gist.github.com/loganjhennessy/c831cb5428c449d60f9be047bd68e82b).

Some more specifics. I'm running the pysc2 command from an Ubuntu 16.04 VM, the replays were generated on Battle.net using the Windows client.

Things I've eliminated:

- Game version miss-match. This is not it. The version of my personal replay was 3.16.1.55958, and I was able to find and run replays of the same version from the replay pack.
- Missing map. Also not it. I tried several replays with different maps, again I verified I could find and run a replay with the same map from  the replay pack.

So, this leaves two things that I can think of right now.

1. Something about attempting to run a replay generated on Windows via the Linux client. But, if this is the case, then how were the replays in the replay pack generated? Obviously those weren't played on Linux, so did they undergo some kind of preprocessing?
2. A flag that must be set when attempting to run a replay that isn't from the replay pack. This will be my next line of investigation.

I guess I'd just like to know if running replays generated from other systems is possible, and if not then is it even possible using the open-source tools that have been provided?

For context, I'd like to build a strategy analyzer app using supervised learning. To analyze someone's strategy, I'll need to accept their replay regardless of what system it was generated on. If I can't do that, it's a bit of a non-starter.

I'll be posting this to the  [Starcraft II API Forum](https://us.battle.net/forums/en/sc2/22814033/) as well.

Thanks.

issue_title: Compat for modules like concurrent.futures which are not bulid-in in python2
issue_body:
Hi!
I found `flake8-import-order` can not work well with some modules which are only built-in in python3 in python2 environment, like `concurrent.futures`. For example:
```
$ cat a.py
import concurrent.futures
import click

$ python --version
Python 2.7.10

$ flake8 a.py 
a.py:1:1: F401 'concurrent.futures' imported but unused
a.py:2:1: F401 'click' imported but unused
```
There is no `I100` warning which should show in python2.

Then I modified `a.py`:
```
$ cat a.py 
import click
import concurrent.futures

$ flake8 a.py 
a.py:1:1: F401 'click' imported but unused
a.py:2:1: F401 'concurrent.futures' imported but unused
a.py:2:1: I100 Import statements are in the wrong order.import concurrent.futures should be before import click
```
There is an `I100` warning which should not show in this import order.

I think it should be more flexible in python2 for these modules like `concurrent.futures`.

Thanks for your patience and sorry for my poor English.

I just added the `forbid_global_loop` parameter to the `asyncio` marker.  Most tests pass fine, I fixed a few others with accidental references to the default event loop.  However, some of them fail in a strange way:

```
asyncio/base_events.py:342: in run_until_complete
    return future.result()
asyncio/futures.py:274: in result
    raise self._exception
asyncio/tasks.py:239: in _step
    result = coro.send(value)
gitmesh/storage.py:35: in check_output
    loop=loop,
asyncio/subprocess.py:197: in create_subprocess_shell
    stderr=stderr, **kwds)
asyncio/base_events.py:940: in subprocess_shell
    protocol, cmd, True, stdin, stdout, stderr, bufsize, **kwargs)
asyncio/unix_events.py:179: in _make_subprocess_transport
    with events.get_child_watcher() as watcher:
asyncio/events.py:604: in get_child_watcher
    return get_event_loop_policy().get_child_watcher()
_ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ 

self = <pytest_asyncio.plugin.ForbiddenEventLoopPolicy object at 0x103dbba20>

    def get_child_watcher(self):
        "Get the watcher for child processes."
>       raise NotImplementedError
E       NotImplementedError
```

Seems like, at least on OSX, the `asyncio` subprocess facilities depend on an undocumented `get_child_watcher()` method of the event loop policy.

I'm guessing part of this problem exceeds the scope of this project: custom event loops must conform to an undocumented interface to be able to support subprocesses.  I'm guessing this needs to be discussed with asyncio maintainers.

However, I guess you'll also want to do something about this in this library because `pytest_asyncio.plugin.ForbiddenEventLoopPolicy` currently doesn't implement this hidden interface.

Hello everyone!

I'm not sure if that's even a `pytest-asyncio` bug, it might go down up to `asyncio`, but Im sharing the details in case it is. I'm trying to use `pytest-asyncio` with `pyppeteer` - a package the wrap Google's Puppetter API and interacts with Chrome. Here's and example test:


```
from pyppeteer import launch
import pytest

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_some_asyncio_code():
    browser = await launch()
    page = await browser.newPage()
    raise Exception('Unexpected Exception')
    await browser.close()
```

The following makes `pytest` freeze, or to be exact - the process is sleeping forever while the C method `epoll_wait` is waiting for any interrupt. 

The following code seems to work-around it:

```
from pyppeteer import launch
import pytest

@pytest.yield_fixture
def loop():
     loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
     asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)
     yield loop

def test_some_asyncio_code(loop):
    async def inner():
        browser = await launch()
        page = await browser.newPage()
        raise Exception('Unexpected Exception')
        await browser.close()

    loop.run_until_complete(inner())
```

The difference here is that I do not close the loop, and Python does it for me when the run ends. Adding 'loop.close()` to the fixture, prints an error that `The event loop is already closed`, which might be a hint of what's happening here?

I'm writing an automated script to install the latest SDK 1.0.0 Preview for Debian/Ubuntu, and I noticed this when performing an `apt-cache search apt-cache search dotnet-dev-1.0.0-preview2` search:

```shell
# apt-cache search dotnet-dev-1.0.0-preview2
dotnet-dev-1.0.0-preview2-003121 - Microsoft .NET Core 1.0.0 - SDK Preview 2
dotnet-dev-1.0.0-preview2-003131 - Microsoft .NET Core 1.0.1 - SDK 1.0.0 Preview 2-003131
dotnet-dev-1.0.0-preview2-003156 - Microsoft .NET Core 1.0.3 - SDK 1.0.0 Preview 2-003156
dotnet-dev-1.0.0-preview2-1-003177 - Microsoft .NET Core 1.1.0 - SDK 1.0.0 Preview 2.1-003177
dotnet-dev-1.0.0-preview2.1-003155 - Microsoft .NET Core 1.1.0 Preview1 - SDK 1.0.0 Preview 2.1-003155
```

Looking at builds `003155` and `003177`, the package name formats are slightly different:

```
# preview2.1 with a period
dotnet-dev-1.0.0-preview2.1-003155
# preview2-1 with a slash.
dotnet-dev-1.0.0-preview2-1-003177
```

I know it seems minute, it was something I noticed when I called `sort` in Bash.

Here was the workaround for it in Bash:
```bash
dotnet=(); for i in $(apt-cache search dotnet-dev-1.0.0-preview2 | awk '{print $1}'); do dotnet+=(`echo $i| rev`); done; IFS=$'\n' sorted=($(sort <<<"${dotnet[*]}")); echo ${sorted[@]} | rev
```

issue_title: Security of .net core 3 clients apps is now delegated to the vendors?
issue_body: Hi, This is a question about client security. How is client security handled in .Core 3.x ? In .NET classic the is all handled in Windows via Windows updates which works pretty well. I mean, if you look at the slew of security patches MS released the last few years with minor and major security issues. Like [this one](https://portal.msrc.microsoft.com/en-US/security-guidance/advisory/CVE-2018-0786) for example. The reason that I asked this question is that vendors/developers who in the future distribute there .NET software with the Net Core 3 framework could expose there clients to an enormous risk when a security breach in .Net Core happens. This could happen because MS is not patching anymore (which is kind of one of MS goals). **For example:** I, as a software vendor, distributes xaml .Net 4.x software to > 200 clients (some major companies). .Net security patches gets fixed by MS so all is good. My clients do not need to worry because MS watches the .Net security space. If I update the application to .Net core 3 this is not the case anymore? Do I need to watch the security bulletins about .Net and update all the time ? If this is the case I see a potential big risk because I (an many other vendors with me) don't want to do this (takes too much time, effort) and the risk is to great when it is forgotten. Companies will also not buy my software anymore. Maybe i'm wrong but it looks like the strategy regarding this one is very flawed and a disaster in the making.

Is there any support for WPF application in .Net Core 2.0 or is it on roadmap?

If no, then what is the other option to make WPF application cross platform?

**Main purpose:**
To make existing WPF application as cross platform(especially for Linux).

Gentoo provide a feature called sets. Letsencrypt  could use it to put dependency in.
This patch will create a set named letsencrypt-prerequisites with letsencrypt dep. And install it.

In this way it's easy  to find waht package was installed by letsencrypt.

```
diff --git a/bootstrap/_gentoo_common.sh b/bootstrap/_gentoo_common.sh
index f49dc00..d1c0e47 100755
--- a/bootstrap/_gentoo_common.sh
+++ b/bootstrap/_gentoo_common.sh
@@ -1,14 +1,13 @@
 #!/bin/sh
 
-PACKAGES="
-  dev-lang/python:2.7
-  dev-python/virtualenv
-  dev-util/dialog
-  app-admin/augeas
-  dev-libs/openssl
-  dev-libs/libffi
-  app-misc/ca-certificates
-  virtual/pkgconfig"
+PACKAGES="dev-lang/python:2.7
+dev-python/virtualenv
+dev-util/dialog
+app-admin/augeas
+dev-libs/openssl
+dev-libs/libffi
+app-misc/ca-certificates
+virtual/pkgconfig"
 
 case "$PACKAGE_MANAGER" in
   (paludis)
@@ -18,6 +17,8 @@ case "$PACKAGE_MANAGER" in
     pmerge --noreplace $PACKAGES
     ;;
   (portage|*)
-    emerge --noreplace $PACKAGES
+    mkdir -p /etc/portage/sets/
+    echo -e "$PACKAGES" > /etc/portage/sets/letsencrypt-prerequisites
+    emerge --noreplace -p @letsencrypt-prerequisites
     ;;
 esac

```

Hi,

this is an idea of how to fix issue #1015. It would be useful in situations where letsencrypt cannot be run on the server in question. A new commandline option `--manual-pipecmd` would receive an argument like:

    'ssh myserver "cat > /tmp/letsencrypt/public_html/%TOKENPATH%"'

When executing `letsencrypt --authenticator manual` with above value for the `--manual-pipecmd` option, the manual plugin could first replace the placeholder `%TOKENPATH%` by the desired path to the token (like `.well-known/acme-challenge/XXXXXXXX`) and then execute this command and pass the content of the token into its standard input.

This way, the user would be able to forge whatever program they need to copy the token into the right remote location on their webserver where they use whatever method they desire to serve its content.

Together with the fix for issue #1125 this would allow complete automation of the certification process without running letsencrypt on the server while at the same time allowing any possible server setup.

issue_title: Make dependency on a2enmod & a2dismod optional
issue_body:
At the moment, the client makes use of a2enmod and a2dismod for enabling some mods in [configurator.py](https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/master/letsencrypt-apache/letsencrypt_apache/configurator.py#L140). But these scripts are [Debian (based distro's) only](http://manpages.debian.org/cgi-bin/man.cgi?query=a2dismod). At least: those scripts are unavailable on Gentoo.

Therefore, the Apache plugin is unavailable too.

It's not unlikely the required mods are already enabled on the server. As far as I can tell, Let's Encrypt just enables [mod_ssl](https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/master/letsencrypt-apache/letsencrypt_apache/configurator.py#L544), [mod_headers](https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/master/letsencrypt-apache/letsencrypt_apache/configurator.py#L820) and [mod_rewrite](https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/master/letsencrypt-apache/letsencrypt_apache/configurator.py#L890). But perhaps I'm missing something :neutral_face: 

Those are not very uncommon modules at all if I'm not mistaken. I would very much like to see some kind of check: if the required modules are already properly enabled, then the dependency on a2enmod and a2dismod could be dropped.

issue_title: AvroProducer key schema must be record
issue_body:
Looking at [Schema Registry quickstart](https://github.com/confluentinc/schema-registry) there doesn't seem to be restriction of having a record type as Kafka key:

> $ curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json" \
>     --data '{"schema": "{\"type\": \"string\"}"}' \
>     http://localhost:8081/subjects/Kafka-key/versions
>   {"id":1}

Yet AvroProducer for some reason [only accepts record type](https://github.com/confluentinc/confluent-kafka-python/blob/master/confluent_kafka/avro/__init__.py#L110).

Would it be reasonable to relax this so we can pass any valid top level avro type, or even make key serialization optional so that only the value part be required to be an avro?

So I configured my vimrc to use python3

This was the file:

TypeError: _getvolumepathname() argument 1 must be str, not bytes
```

turns out it was an issue with python3 using bytes instead of strings for some things now...
So tl;dr I changed these lines of code and it fixed the issue on my end (blocks/lines I added are commented)

IN: .\powerline\lib\vcs\\_\_init\_\_.py:
```
def generate_directories(path):
	#Converts path from 'bytes' to 'str' object
	path = path.decode('utf-8')
	if os.path.isdir(path):
		#Converts path back into bytes and sends it out to wherever it needs to go.
		yield bytes(path, 'utf-8')
	while True:
		if os.path.ismount(path):
			break
		old_path = path
		path = os.path.dirname(path)
		if path == old_path or not path:
			break
		#Converts path back into bytes and sends it out to wherever it needs to go.
		yield bytes(path, 'utf-8')
```

IN: .\powerline\lib\shell.py:
```
def readlines(cmd, cwd):
	'''Run command and read its output, line by line

	:param list cmd:
		Command which will be run.
	:param str cwd:
		Working directory of the command which will be run.
	'''
	
	#Converts every 'bytes' object in cmd into a str object
	cmd = list(cmd)
	for i in range(len(cmd)):
		if type(cmd[i]) == type(bytes('', 'utf-8')):
			cmd[i] = cmd[i].decode()
	cmd = tuple(cmd)
	
	#converts cwd to 'str' object
	cwd = cwd.decode()
	
	p = Popen(cmd, shell=False, stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE, cwd=cwd)
	encoding = get_preferred_input_encoding()
	p.stderr.close()
	with p.stdout:
		for line in p.stdout:
			yield line[:-1].decode(encoding)
```

I'm sure there are more elegant ways of doing this (and that is why I am not issuing a pull request) but feel free to make any modifications to and/or use this solution in your codebase if you want. 👍

issue_title: How do you profile applications that use multiprocessing?
issue_body:
I have a very, very parallelized project, that uses python multiprocessing extensively.

Is there a canonical way to use vmprof for profiling applications that have more then one process? Right now, you only get the main thread profile data.

I wonder if there's a way to monkey patch multiprocessing to install the profiler in all created processes. 

Also, is there a way to join multiple profile output files? It'd be ideal if you could have one file writer process that aggregated all the sampled process at runtime, but I can also see having each thread dump it's own profile log file, that then gets aggregated once your application has completed.

I think that in marshmallow swagger, some pathological cases are not handled the best way.

This

```python
        field = fields.Str(validate=[
            validate.Equal('only choice'),
            validate.Equal('only choice_2')])
```
produces

```
    ['enum'] = ['only choice', 'only_choice_2']
```

which is wrong. Admittedly, the field declaration does not make much sense.

Also

```python
        field = fields.Str(validate=[
            validate.Equal('only choice'),
            validate.OneOf(['freddie', 'brian', 'john'])])
```

produces

```
    ['enum'] = ['only choice']
```

which is wrong too. This field declaration is a big dodgy as well.

Those are corner cases that might happen, perhaps, in some complex inheritance scenarios.

apispec should at least return the correct output. Maybe raising a warning is out of scope, as if we engage in consistency validation in apispec, this could end up being a lot of work.

No time and interest to address this right now but I thought I'd leave a note here.

Copied from https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/issues/153#issuecomment-140229473:

There are a few improvements to the pre/post_* and validates* decorators I'd like to make before the 2.0.0 final release.

Problems:

- As pointed out in #216, there is no way to get access to the target object (the data to be (de)serialized). This is possible, however, with validates_schema via the pass_original param.
- Also related to #216: The "raw" param is misleading. Users might think that it means that the raw target object is passed, but it does not.

Proposal:

- Rename raw -> pass_many.
- Add pass_original to post_dump and post_load. Maybe rename to pass_target or pass_input--not sure about this.

Update: `pass_original` only makes sense for `post_dump` and `post_load`.

issue_title: Proposals to improve Windows 10 open exception
issue_body:
The WIndows 10 driver seems more thorough and more fussier than Windows 7, such that my application successfully opens a serial port to a custom device (using an STM32 microcontroller with integrated USB device) when using Windows 7, but fails (raises an exception) when using Windows 10.  The same code works perfectly on latest macOS too.  It's just Windows 10 that barfs.

#258 has some notes/comments.  In summary the problem is the STM32 code does not return the correct comm settings back when requested.  It appears the Win 10 driver sends the comm settings, then reads them back from the device to ensure they are correct.  Not unreasonable, but it causes an issue in real life.

The ideal fix is to fix the STM32 code, however in my case (and possibly others), there are large(ish) number of these products out in the wild and the bootloader firmware is not user upgradeable :(

My only choice is to fix it at the PC software end (i.e. using PySerial in my case).  I would like feedback on the following options, so that I can produce a PR that the PySerial maintainers are likely to adopt (I really don't want to maintain my own fork).

1. Add a constructor/initialiser parameter (e.g. `ignore_open_error`) to ignore the error code of `SetCommState()` in `_reconfigure_port()` (line 221).
` if not win32.SetCommState(self._port_handle, ctypes.byref(comDCB)):`)

2. Be able to catch the exception in my own code and be able to continue on with the initialisaiton.  My thoughts were to be able to derive my own class to do this.  I haven't tried it, but maybe it would be as simple as overload the `_reconfigure_port()` function, by calling `super()._reconfigure()` around a `try:`/`except:` ?  Would that work?  I could also implement option 1 as well.

Option 2, if it works, would let me use stock standard PySerial, but it wont help others, unless they use the same work around.

Any thoughts or advice ???

Hey,

I'm developing using Python 2.7 but a user uses 2.6.

Some Modules throw errors because the usage of the str.format() function:
On Python 2.7 an index between the {} is optional, on 2.6 it is not.

I'm on my work PC without git so i can't submit the fix, and I also can't append a zip so you could merge, so sadly I can't do my part beside hinting you.

To fix:
Open IDE, search for every '{}' and replace with '{0}' (or '{0} - {1}') where needed.

Eg:
Your __str__ from list_ports_common.py:

    def __str__(self):
        return '{} - {}'.format(self.device, self.description)

My version:
    def __str__(self):
        return '{0} - {1}'.format(self.device, self.description)

I love your work! Keep it up!

Ok, so I worked around that, and started on replicating https://apple.github.io/turicreate/docs/userguide/image_classifier/introduction.html to make sure everything is working properly before I go through the work of gathering real training data.

issue_body:

  ```
  def prepend_urls(self):

            url(r"^(?P<resource_name>%s)/me/?$" % self._meta.resource_name, self.wrap_view('me')),
            ....
        ]

    def me(self, request, *args, **kwargs):
        self.is_authenticated(request)
        kwargs['id'] = request.user.id
        return self.dispatch_detail(request, **kwargs)
```

And it stopped working in last tastypie release, and was working in 13.1. In latest tastypie it just returns:

issue_title: Python documentation of model test is not correct
issue_body: In python documentation of model test (https://bigdl-project.github.io/0.2.0/#APIGuide/Module/ and https://bigdl-project.github.io/master/#APIGuide/Module/), the API example is not correct. The original example code is: evaluateResult = model.test(testSet, 1, [Top1Accuracy]). It should be evaluateResult = model.test(testSet, 1, [Top1Accuracy()]).

m = SpatialConvolution(n_input_plane,n_output_plane,kernel_w,kernel_h,stride_w=1,stride_h=1,pad_w=0,pad_h=0,n_group=1,propagate_back=True,init_method="default")
```

issue_title: Serialization to support other tensor types
issue_body:
Currently only Float/Double tensor are supported in serialization, With TF change, we need to support other tensor types in serialization

All tensor types are listed below


object BooleanType extends TensorDataType

object CharType extends TensorDataType

object StringType extends TensorDataType

object IntType extends TensorDataType

object ShortType extends TensorDataType

issue_title: add a function that multiplies voltages and currents for arrays
issue_body:
I'd like to consider adding a function to ``pvsystem.py`` that would multiply the voltages and currents returned by ``pvsystem.sapm`` and ``pvsystem.singlediode``. Of course, the motivation for this is to make it easier to calculate array parameters.

```python
def multiply_voltage_current(data, voltage_multiplier=1, current_multiplier=1): 
    # as written, only works with a DataFrame
    # could make it work with a dict, but it would be more verbose
    data = data.copy()
    voltages = ['v_mp', 'v_oc']
    currents = ['i_mp' ,'i_x', 'i_xx', 'i_sc']
    data[voltages] *= voltage_multiplier
    data[currents] *= current_multiplier
    data['p_mp'] *= voltage_multiplier * current_multiplier
    
    return data
    
    
# within the PVSystem class...

    def multiply_voltage_current(self, data):
     
        return multiply_voltage_current(
            data, voltage_multiplier=self.series_modules,
            current_multiplier=self.parallel_modules)
```

Maybe the function can also be reused for some system losses.

issue_title: piu.yaml MUST have a priority over autodetection
issue_body:
#34 and #35 have changed old and absolutely valid behavior:
* if odd-host isn't passed as an argument, piu should take whatever is written in `piu.yaml` and only if config is not there or invalid it should go with autodetection.

issue_title: Support the remaining ID3 iTunes Podcast Tags
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **Gustav Broberg (Bitbucket: [gustav_b](http://bitbucket.org/gustav_b), GitHub: Unknown)**

----------------------------------------

Issue #141 added support for some iTunes podcast ID3 tags:

* `TDES` Podcast Description
* `TGID` Podcast GUID
* `WFED` Podcast URL

But unfortunately the support is not complete because there are three more tags that iTunes use [[1]](http://wiki.hydrogenaud.io/index.php?title=Tag_Mapping#Miscellaneous), that are not supported:

* `TCAT` Podcast Category
* `TKWD` Podcast Keywords
* `PCST` Podcast Flag

The `PCST` flag is what iTunes uses to identify if an MP3 is a podcast, its content is irrelevant, it just need to be present. [[2]](https://github.com/judwhite/IdSharp/pull/1)

It would be great if mutagen/mid3v2 could support reading/writing these frames too so that podcasts can be properly tagged for iTunes.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/lazka/mutagen/issue/249

issue_title: What is the rationale behind Z3_parse_smtlib2_file's interface?
issue_body:
The interface of `Z3_parse_smtlib2_file()` looks like this

```c++
    Z3_ast Z3_API Z3_parse_smtlib2_file(Z3_context c, Z3_string file_name,
                                        unsigned num_sorts,
                                        Z3_symbol const sort_names[],
                                        Z3_sort const sorts[],
                                        unsigned num_decls,
                                        Z3_symbol const decl_names[],
                                        Z3_func_decl const decls[]);
```

There are some oddities here?

* Why do the `num_sorts`, `sort_names`, `sorts`, etc.. arguments exist? At a quick glance I thought they were outputs (i.e. the sorts and declarations from the smtlib2 file) but in fact looking at the implementation these are inputs. They seems to be used to populate the internal Z3 context before parsing the smtlib2 file. 
What is the reason for this and when is it useful?

* The function returns a single `Z3_ast` with all assertions conjuncted. Wouldn't a `Z3_ast_vector` make more sense?

issue_title: Use ; instead of , as command separator
issue_body:
MPSYT is currently using `,` both in its commands, and as a command separator when inputting commands as command-line arguments, I suggest we replace this separator with the `;` sign.

As it stand now, if one wants to search for `abc` then play lets say song 1 and 3 the current command for that is `$ mpsyt "/abc, 1,,3"`. I think this syntax is pretty unintuitive. The biggest problem is that we have to introduce the double comma hack in order to get around using comma as a separator, when a much easier solution would just to use a different glyph than comma for separating commands.

I suggest using semi colon as it most likely will not show up in users free text searches, any of our commands and it is a natural command delimiter since it is widely used in many programming languages.

issue_title: Update VIIRS L1B reader to support new standard file naming scheme
issue_body:
#### Problem description

The NASA official VIIRS L1B filenaming scheme has now been finalized and has been changed from what SatPy previously supported. See https://ladsweb.modaps.eosdis.nasa.gov/api/v1/productPage/product=VNP02MOD

A coworker provided me with the following mapping:

```
VNP02MOD -> L1B moderate resolution SNPP
VNP03MOD -> GEO moderate resolution SNPP
VNP02IMG -> L1B imager resolution SNPP
VNP03IMG -> GEO imager resolution SNPP 
VNP02DNB -> L1B day night band SNPP
VNP03DNB -> GEO day night band SNPP
```

Note that the ESDTs for JPSS-1 will be very similar except the NP gets switched to J1 so:
```
VJ102MOD
VJ103MOD
VJ102IMG
VJ103IMG
VJ102DNB
VJ103DNB
```

I think this should just require updating the file pattern in `viirs_l1b.yaml`.

issue_title: incomplete object errors constantly spamming terminal
issue_body:
I suspect there is a minor bug in the Monitoring loop and possibly the flushing algorithm in the Registry.

At the moment I have several jobs which rely on a Ganga plugin in order to fully load the XML file.

The expected behaviour is that the file should fail to load asif the XML is corrupt and I be notified about this error and Ganga should move on with other things.

However I'm constantly (once per 2-3 seconds) being spammed with errors akin to:
```
ERROR    #1 Error(s) Loading File: /afs/cern.ch/user/r/rcurrie/gangadir/repository/rcurrie/LocalXML/6.0/jobs/0xxx/260/data~
ERROR    XML File failed to load for Job id: 260
ERROR    Actual Error was:
Repository 'jobs' object #260 is not accessible because of an InaccessibleObjectError: Repository 'jobs' object #260 is not accessible because of an PluginManagerError: PluginManagerError:  (PluginManagerError(...), u"cannot find 'Im3ShapeApp' in a category 'applications', or elsewhere")
ERROR    Error Loading Jobs! '[260]'

ERROR    XML File failed to load for Job id: 260
```

This is reduced significantly with the monitoring turned off so I suspect the monitoring is no longer blacklisting jobs it can't load correctly but it doesn't go away and other than the monitoring loop I think only the registry flushing loop is the only active task dealing with handling objects which may be on disk behind the scenes.

I can just about work with this as long as it doesn't get any worse but this should probably be looked into even though it doesn't effect users.

issue_title: Gauss Support is needed for GaudiRun
issue_body:
Support for Gauss and the GausSplitter is needed in GaudiRun.

The original implementation pickles the resulting python file and passes it to `gaudirun.py`. This means an additional level of work is needed to find out what exactly (and in what order) various options are passed to the running Job.

I'm aware of at least 1 minor bug in the original Gauss+GaussSplitter implementation which has been found by a PhD student trying to get their analysis working where there is some minor disagreement between different parts of the code to the number of jobs being generated.

In the original implementation there is 1 pickle file per subjob which means a unique inputsandbox.
I'm keen to explore passing the unique options here as runtime script arguments which would mean that submitting many subjobs should be much quicker.
(This may require reassembling a pickle object on the WN, I don't yet know)

An LHCb user has spotted that the job merger appears to be broken and I can confirm the error they see:
```
GangaAttributeError:  ('merger: attempt to assign an incompatible object <Ganga.Lib.Mergers.Merger.RootMerger object at 0x7f2cb0240410> to the property in category "mergers found cat: postprocessor"',)
```

I've had a look at the Merger tests and it appears we don't actually have a test which assigns a merger to a job and tests if it ran.

We should probably add this ASAP as this seems like a fairly big oversight.

I suspect this is likely related to the removal of ``___construct__`` but I'm surprised it's gone unreported for a few versions in this case. (Possibly due to summer).

I'll report back if a workaround if found,

Rob

issue_title: Need documentation on how to run FEFF
issue_body:
All the documentation is about running VASP workflows

An atomate author that is running / using the FEFF workflows needs to add documentation on how to set up and run FEFF.

The Voronoi-tessellation-based featurizers all have a minor error rate due to problematic structures failing to tessellate.

As an example, `mp-623782` fails to tessellate due to an error running qhull. 

There are a few possible routes we could take to trying to lower the error rate:
1. Making sure our cutoff for Voronoi tessellations is large enough to capture all NNs
2. Perturbing the unit cell and re-attempting the tessellation (suggestion from Bryce Meredig).

If these don't work, we should at least report actionable error messages (e.g., "tessellation failed, consider removing this entry from the dataset or turning on `ignore_errors`)

issue_title: Allow user to select configuration file
issue_body:
Pulp Smash's configuration file does not live with the source code itself. This has the advantage that Pulp Smash can be installed in one of many ways, yet the test suite can still be called in an indentical manner. It doesn't matter whether you:

* Install system-wide by executing `sudo pip install pulp_smash` or `sudo python setup.py install`
* Install on a per-user basis with `python setup.py install --user`
* Install into a virtualenv
* Run directly from source code

In all of these cases, you can execute the exact same command to run the test suite: `python -m unittest discover pulp_smash.tests`. And in all of these cases, the same config file can be used. There's no need to do something crazy like `sudo vim /usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/pulp_smash/settings.json`!

There's one down-side to the current approach, though: it's hard to work with multiple configuration files. Imagine that a user creates two virtualenvs, installs Pulp Smash into each, and then wants to run Pulp Smash from both locations. How can the user make the two Pulp Smash installations use a different configuration file? Right now, it's impossible to do without resorting to something like this:

```sh
vim ~/tmp/settings.json
XDG_CONFIG_DIRS='~/tmp/' python -m unittest2 discover pulp_smash.tests
```

And that *works*, but gosh darn it's kinda terrible.

Let's add proper support for multiple configuration files. The end goal is the ability to execute commands like this:

```sh
python -m pulp_smash run --config ~/tmp/settings.json
python -m pulp_smash run --config pulp_servers.json
python -m pulp_smash run --config pulp_nodes.json
```

The first command would make Pulp Smash use `~/tmp/settings.json`. The second and third commands would make Pulp Smash search through the `$XDG_CONFIG_DIRS` for files named `pulp_servers.json` and `pulp_nodes.json`, respectively. If this procedure sound confusing, just think of how the shell executes commands. If you type `/usr/bin/df`, the shell executes `/usr/bin/df`. If you type `df`, the shell searches through each path in `$PATH` and uses the first executable named `df` it finds.

All this assumes that the test suite can be run by executing `python -m pulp_smash run`. That's a whole 'nuther issue.

1. Describe what you expected to happen.

`PulpSmashConfig` doesn't correctly document the default values for its constructor's arguments. Here's `__init__`'s signature:

```python
__init__(self, pulp_auth=None, pulp_version=None, systems=None)
```

And here's the class's documentation:

```
:param pulp_auth: A two-tuple. Credentials to use when communicating with
    the server. For example: ``('username', 'password')``.
:param pulp_version: A string, such as '1.2' or '0.8.rc3'. Defaults to
    '1!0' (epoch 1, version 0). Must be compatible with the `packaging`_
    library's ``packaging.version.Version`` class.
:param pulp_smash.config.PulpSystem systems: A list of the hosts comprising
    a Pulp application.
```

Thesea are my conclusions:

* I have no idea what `PulpSmashConfig().pulp_auth` defaults to.
* I expect that `PulpSmashConfig().pulp_version == Version('1!0')`
* I have no idea what `PulpSmashConfig().systems` defaults to.

2. Describe what actually happened.

What actually happens when `PulpSmashConfig()` is executed? This:

```
>>> from pulp_smash.config import PulpSmashConfig
>>> cfg = PulpSmashConfig()
>>> cfg.pulp_auth is cfg.pulp_version is None
True
>>> cfg.systems
[]
```

This doesn't match my expectations. As a fix, I would expect the following:

* `PulpSmashConfig` clearly states the default value of all attributes.
* `PulpSmashConfig` abides by the stated default values.

It may also be a good idea to move all "default value" logic from `PulpSmashConfig.read` into `PulpSmashConfig.__init__`. DRY an all that.

3. Describe what version of Pulp and what OS is affected by the issue.

n/a

4. State steps to re-create the issue.

see step 2

5. If there is a related pulp.plan.io issue, link to it, and set the "smash test" field.

n/a

6. Choose proper labels. See: [Smash Labels](https://pulp-smash.readthedocs.io/en/latest/about.html#labels)

Done.

        # Remove query args from names. Fixes cache and signature arguments

issue_title: casing issue: Pubsub should be PubSub
issue_body:
Based on the [Google Java Style Guide recommendation on camel case](https://google.github.io/styleguide/javaguide.html#s5.3-camel-case) Pub/Sub should be translated into code as PubSub instead of Pubsub. 

[PubsubMessage](https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/pubsub/v1beta2/pubsub.proto#L116) and the [outerJavaClassName](https://github.com/googleapis/googleapis/blob/master/google/pubsub/v1beta2/pubsub.proto#L23) would need to be refactored to be consistent with the naming unless there is a good reason to deviate from the standard style.
