`csvgrep` output result always makes use of the comma `,` delimeter, regardless of the delimeter specified in the command with `-d` option.
The delimeter is correctly interpreted for the input files.

Example:
`scolon_sep`: input file using semicolon separator
`output`: resulting output from csvgrep

    $ csvgrep -d\; -c 1 -m matchvalue scolon_sep > output

`output` file field separator will always be `,`

issue_title: Module required in py::exception and py::register_exception?
issue_body:
I wonder why `exception` ctor and `register_exception` take `module&` and not just a scope, like `class_`? Is it only to get a nicely formatted name or are there some other reasons?

Sometimes there's no well-defined module -- e.g. you have an external C++ library that provides a way to register pybind11 translators for its exceptions, but doesn't have any pybind11 modules per se. It would be handy to be able to register exception "inline" (kind of like it's done in the implementation of `py::iterator`, the class is created on the fly with `handle()` as the scope).

issue_title: Insecure certificates issued due to common name (csr) scrambling
issue_body:
Upon updating from `letsencrypt-auto` to `certbot-auto`, trying to reinstate my certificates resulted in all but one of them being insecure. Upon further inspection:

`sudo ./certbot-auto certonly --standalone -d example.com -d www.example.com -d example2.com -d www.example2.com`

I realized that *all* of my domains have `example.com` (first `-d` parameter) listed as their common name. Thus, causing the certificates to be invalid. Further, deleting the `~/.local/share/letsencrypt/` and `/etc/letsencrypt` directories and running `certbot-auto` with a different domain name first does not correct the issue. I had to completely roll back the VPS in question (thank goodness for DigitalOcean backups) to remedy the issue.

This never seemed to be an issue with the former `letsencrypt-auto`... does it have to do with `--csr` or simply a bug?

issue_title: If the user picks "cancel" from the Apache vhost selection menu, Certbot doesn't exit
Standalone plugin ignores port I asked to be used
issue_body:
If you're having trouble using Certbot and aren't sure you've found a bug or
request for a new feature, please first try asking for help at
https://community.letsencrypt.org/. There is a much larger community there of
people familiar with the project who will be able to more quickly answer your
questions.

## My operating system is (include version):

    Linux openvpn1.ismyservice.space 4.4.0-134-generic #160-Ubuntu SMP Wed Aug 15 14:58:00 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

    certbot 0.26.1

## I installed Certbot with (certbot-auto, OS package manager, pip, etc):

    Ubuntu package maanger

## I ran this command and it produced this output:

    sudo certbot certonly --standalone --non-interactive  --preferred-challenge http-01 
       --http-01-port 10000 --agree-tos 
       --email realtebo@example.com --domains realtebo.example.com 
       --pre-hook 'sudo service webmin stop' 
       --post-hook 'sudo service webmin start' 
       --staging --dry-run

     Domain: realtebo.example.com
     Type:   connection
     Detail: Fetching
     http://realtebo.example.com/.well-known/acme-challenge/DbE18Iyfud0xBRtrfvz-ILifSzqV-7GoV5uRAyuNYRw:
     Timeout during connect (likely firewall problem)

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:

1. Firewall is opened on port 10000. When certbot ends, it restart webmin, that is running on the same port. I can connect to webmin from outside using port 10000 without any problem

2. See the log in the following point; certbot is starting its standalone webserver on port 10000, and it's ok, but it is using port 80 for the challenge !

If anyone can shed some light on this problem I'd be forever grateful! Long-time Linux, first time certbot sys-admin. - doing by best to follow the issue template prompts here too...

Ubuntu 16.04

Installed certbox with apt-get (suggested in Digital Ocean docs)

## How I'm running certbot
certbot auto renew is setup on a cron-job at 03:10 everyday to check (and potentially renew the certificates) using the apache plugin

## Certbot's behavior differed from what I expected because:

the letsencrypt logs show at 3am and then again at **12 noon and also midnight - why is that?**

and my apache server is being gracefully restarted (assuming by the apache plugin, as I've not manually configured it to) at 06:25 on the dot everyday. 

_I've looked through the certbot apache plugin code here on github and it does execute a graceful restart (as it needs to), but there is no mention of a specific time...? **Why would the server restart at 06.25** (only) everyday when certbot is firing at 03.10 (I would guess a varying delay in restart, based on how long certbot took this time, maybe couple mins, but that doesn't match up with 06.25) - and no idea why certbot is also showing logs checking certificates at the other times... :S_

_I've looked through the crontab, nothing in there - also checked the cron directories and no mention of letsencrypt/certbot or anything that would restart apache at 06.25._

issue_title: Temporarily ignore cryptography deprecation warnings in verify_renewable_cert_sig
issue_body:
At PyCon, one of the cryptography/pyopenssl maintainers switched some code around in  `certbot.crypto_util.verify_renewable_cert_sig` to use cryptography rather than pyopenssl to try and work around some segfaults on some obscure systems. He told me at PyCon the methods he was using because of our need to support ancient versions were triggering deprecation warnings, but we didn't need to worry about them ripping it out anytime soon.

I didn't think anything of it because deprecation warnings aren't usually shown to users, but cryptography use their custom warning type which are shown. We haven't been seeing this because they don't show up in our pinned versions, but you can see it by upgrading cryptography in the venv and running `certbot certificates`.

We should use the `warnings` module to ignore these warnings during the duration of the method.

The variable board will be used in the SConstruct file for building the verilog files for the specified board
Ex:
scons board=icestick

Current board values will be:  icestick, icezum, go-board

If the variable board is set, it will override other fpga flags passed:

Ex:
scons board=icezum fpga_size=8k

It won't set the fpga_size to 8k, because it will use the one determined for the icezum board (1k)

issue_title: [Feature] Add fpga flags to the build command
issue_body:
Ex:   apio build --pack vq100

It will build the current example seting the scons fpga vairable  fpga_pack to vq100

(Internally it will call scons with:  scons fpga_pack=vq100)

The supported build flags are:  --pack, --size and --type, corresponding to the fpga_pack, fpga_size and fpga_type scons flags

This is an advanced feature.  Normal user will create a proyect first indiating the board type and then build it using plain apio build.   But allowing to change the flags directly is very usefull for easily testing new boards

issue_title: bug 1330396 - add a test git repo tarball into scriptworker/test/data
issue_body:
Currently [two of our tests](https://github.com/mozilla-releng/scriptworker/blob/4d6fb6f67ef8f94676b41755f3c325e30d87cca9/scriptworker/test/test_gpg.py#L593-L605), `test_get_git_revision` and `test_get_latest_tag`, use the current scriptworker git repo for tests.

This is poor for at least two reasons:
1. because we don't know what each person's git repo status will be when they run the tests, we have to run another git command to find the latest revision or the latest tag.  It isn't deterministic.
2. it isn't possible to run the tests without the .git subdir, e.g. from a pip tarball.  The dockerfile currently pushes the .git subdir to the container so we can run unit tests.

Instead, let's

a. create a test git repo, with a few revisions, and a few tags.
b. tar it up and add it to `scriptworker/test/data/`
c. change those two tests to extract that tarball into a tmpdir, and then running the tests against that tmpdir.

we can then have a deterministic latest tag and latest revision.

Sometimes some script or command will return non-zero status but we do not want such errors to interrupt the whole process. One way to do this is to

```
try:
    run('''script''')
except:
   pass
```

but this makes the script less easy to read.

Another method is to 

```
run:
    cmd_that_might_fail || true
```

and makes the command always return true, but this method is not portable.

An option `allow_error` would be nice to translate all errors from actions to warning.

Every clean-cut rule can backshot because real use cases are diverse.

Because of the `-r` option, a cell needs to be self-contained so that it can be executed as a separate script on the remote host. It is therefore natural to execute the script with a clean namespace, which would also avoid some confusion as the source of variables in the script (could be from surrounding cells, from command line etc).

However, executing cells like this breaks the continuity of the Jupyter notebook as variables calculated above could not be used. Since the only way to pass a variable to the script is through parameters, users would have to do

```
%run --var value
parameter var
```
and this only works for simple parameters, not complex data types such as pandas dataframe. It then makes a bit sense to allow some way to pass variables from SoS namespace to the script....

We have not had any trouble in signatures because we always assumed a single file system. Currently if the other process (`task`) is handling the execution, the signature is passed to that process and that process is responsible for writing the signature.

Things have changed if we allow remote execution. Because local and remote files will be different, it is no longer possible to use the same signature. What we can do is use both remote and local signatures

1. The local process creates a signature
2. The remote process creates a signature
3. The remote process writes its own signature
4. The local process writes a local signature after it receives results from the remote server.

During re-evaluation, the local sos could skip a step (does not even know if the task is executed remotely) and the remote server could skip evaluation based on its own signature.

Bo

Not sure if others also have this problem but I have found myself frequently need to go back to the front of the notebook to rerun a few cells to initialize the analysis or reset some variables so that I can start a new piece of analysis from fresh. This happens after

1. incorrect statements that mess up these variables
2. need to reset and start a new analysis
3. need to make sure certain key variables are correct

I am therefore wondering if we should facilitate such actions by something like

1. Mark cells that should be re-evaluated during reset.
2. Introduce a `%reset` magic to execute cells that are marked.

For 1, we can use the existing tagging mechanism so users can manually add a tag (`init`?) to such cells.
For 2, we could do `%restart --reset`, or `%dict --reset` or a dedicated `%reset` magic, but the behavior needs to be well-defined because we could restart subkernels, restart sos, reset sos dict in addition to running marked cells.

issue_title: Sample_weights - question about what happens, improve documentation
issue_body:
After sample_weights were introduced in response to [this](https://github.com/EpistasisLab/tpot/issues/310), the documentation for sample_weights was updated with this description:

> Per-sample weights. Higher weights force TPOT to put more emphasis on those points.

This leaves some questions unanswered, and I want to make sure I understand what is happening "under the hood". 

From looking at [operator_utils.py]..........(https://github.com/EpistasisLab/tpot/blob/master/tpot/operator_utils.py),

```python
def set_sample_weight(pipeline_steps, sample_weight=None):
    """Recursively iterates through all objects in the pipeline and sets sample weight.
    Parameters
    ----------
    pipeline_steps: array-like
        List of (str, obj) tuples from a scikit-learn pipeline or related object
    sample_weight: array-like
        List of sample weight
    Returns
    -------
    sample_weight_dict:
        A dictionary of sample_weight
    """
    sample_weight_dict = {}
    if not isinstance(sample_weight, type(None)):
        for (pname, obj) in pipeline_steps:
            if inspect.getargspec(obj.fit).args.count('sample_weight'):
                step_sw = pname + '__sample_weight'
                sample_weight_dict[step_sw] = sample_weight

    if sample_weight_dict:
        return sample_weight_dict
    else:
        return None
```
 ......it seems that each element in the pipeline is checked to see if it has a `.fit()` method that accepts a sample_weight argument.

If so, I have two questions regarding "missing opportunities" to pass sample_weights.

1) it might miss passing sample_weights to `fit_transform()` (but hopefully tpot never uses `fit_transform` and instead simply calls `fit` and `transform` separately)
2) it might miss passing sample_weights to some function that could use it, although I don't know of any (and there might not be any)

Also I wanted to know if sample_weights is used in tpot's scoring function, which determines preferences between pipelines?

Finally, maybe another sentence or two in the documentation to explain briefly what is happening with sample_weights would be helpful to future people having these same questions.

Thanks for tpot! Wonderful seeing this project progress over the last two years.

issue_title: Add Callbacks for logging and/or performing custom operations after every generation
issue_body: Similar to Keras's [“callbacks”](https://keras.io/callbacks/)— we need add a class that can access the training state after every training iteration for better logging and/or performing custom operations after every generation during TPOT optimization. This class is related to a few features in current version (0.9.2) of TPOT (e.g. early stopping and verbosity in general).

issue_title: Be smarter about detecting changes from --command
issue_body:
See https://github.com/drdoctr/doctr/pull/266. Prior to that PR, if --command changes a file that isn't synced, it is either ignored, or in the case where no files are actually added by the sync, errors. After that PR, it doesn't error. 

I'm not so sure about auto-adding new files from --command. The command line docs for the flag do say they need to be added manually. But changed files should most likely be detected and added. Perhaps we just need to do an "add all" when --command is used. Or we could do a before and after diff?

Running `pulumi { preview, update, destroy }` makes a lot of API calls to the Pulumi Service, such as every time a log line is rendered, and for every change that is made to the checkpoint file.

The latency added by each API call update process can add many seconds to the overall update. Most of the time this is masked by the much longer delay in actually modifying cloud resources. But for previews which are persisted in the service, it can increase the duration by a large factor.

This issue tracks improving the performance of `pulumi preview` with regard to the overhead of communicating with the service.

issue_title: Plasma manager blocks on an object transfer when the object is evicted.
issue_body:
Plasma managers request object transfers from each other. These requests get queued at each Plasma manager, which sends the object data to the receiver in order. It's possible for the requested object to get evicted while the request is waiting in the queue. In this case, the sending Plasma manager will block (possibly forever) trying to get the object.

This can be seen in the multinode reconstruction tests: [logs](https://api.travis-ci.org/jobs/200109761/log.txt?deansi=true).

We should fix this by having the Plasma manager skip requests for which the object is no longer available locally. The receiving Plasma manager should be notified that the transfer is canceled, if some of the object data had already been sent.

issue_title: Separate worker log files from local scheduler log files.
issue_body:
Currently, STDOUT and STDERR for the workers is directed to the STDOUT and STDERR of the local scheduler (which is in `/tmp/raylogs/local_scheduler_*`). This happens because the local scheduler creates the workers, but it makes the output difficult to read.

The function to edit is `start_worker` in `src/local_scheduler/local_scheduler.cc`.

We should do something like this http://stackoverflow.com/questions/5517913/redirecting-stdout-to-file-after-a-fork.

So the question here is what to do with the bank. Currently in V2 the bank is accessible only through the Economy cog itself, which makes it a hassle to get to and means any cog that depends on the bank automatically depends on Economy. This is an issue because we have undefined load order of cogs and tbh some people just may not want Economy.

### Proposed Solution 1
V3 Downloader has implemented a little know feature for shared libraries. We can make use of this for the bank and make it available to every cog with a single import:

`from cog_shared import bank`

### Proposed Solution 2
We can make the bank available from within core Red which means that it would be accessible with this line:

`from core import bank`

### Secondary problem
In Palm's Economy rewrite he currently has the bank implemented as a cog with a few commands (to change bank name, currency name, make it global). Should the bank remain with those commands or should they be implemented in Economy (and as a consequence, all alternative economies)?

### Thoughts?

issue_title: No styling change when widget is disabled
issue_body:
Prior to ipywidgets 6, when a widget was disabled its style was changed so that it looked faded, as shown in the pic below. Starting with ipywidgets 6 when `disabled=True` the widget is disabled but the style does not change.

![button-disable](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1147167/27297874-38396694-54ec-11e7-85b7-e82ac735d05f.gif)

As discussed in maartenbreddels/ipyvolume#41, the embedding script should insist on specific javascript library versions.

Furthermore, I think it would be desirable to have an `offline=True` keyword in the ipywidgets/embed.py functions, such that all JavaScript package dependancies were exported to an accompanying folder. The html/js could then be used in a similar manner to standard media formats (.jpeg, .mp4,...), i.e. opened offline and (relatively) resistant to future, external changes (for instance changes to url names).

issue_title: Creating a Persistence Volume (PV) is not automatically creating related `nfs` path directory
Unable to deserialize models with attributes of type RuntimeRawExtension
issue_body:
Minimal example for ControllerRevision (requires an active kubernetes cluster with working kubeconfig):
```python
from kubernetes import client, config

config.load_kube_config()
apps_v1beta1_api = client.AppsV1beta1Api()
controller_revisions = apps_v1beta1_api.list_controller_revision_for_all_namespaces()
```

Issue seems to be here: https://github.com/kubernetes-client/python/blob/master/kubernetes/client/api_client.py#L617

```python
 if data is not None \
             -->  and klass.attribute_map[attr] in data \
                   and isinstance(data, (list, dict)):
    value = data[klass.attribute_map[attr]]
    kwargs[attr] = self.__deserialize(value, attr_type)
```
where it is looking for the `'Raw'` attribute in `data`, but `data` is a freeform JSON object with no specific `'Raw'` key. Because this conditional fails, nothing is ever added to kwargs, and `RuntimeRawExtension` is initialized without the required `raw` value.

issue_title: RecordLink.blocker and Gazetteer.blocker create a huge number of blocks
issue_body:
Both RecordLink.blocker and Gazetteer.blocker produce a huge number of blocks, which makes them practically unusable.

I use a dedupe version downloaded on May 2017 (not sure about the exact version number).
Python version is 3.5.3 and the server is RHEL 6.5 .

Some code for reproducing the problem is attached. It's based on pgsql_big_dedupe_example.
Please see the "instructions.txt" file for instructions.

[Blocker problem.zip](https://github.com/dedupeio/dedupe/files/1100245/Blocker.problem.zip)

It demonstrates how RecordLink.blocker generates over 200 affiliations to blocks per donor in average, 
 while Gazetteer.blocker generates even more affiliations by far.
Compared to that, Dedupe.blocker only generates about 1.5 affiliations to blocks per donor.

A few notes:

  1. They all use the same JSON training file, with about 50 "yes" and 50 "no" records. 

  2. Since pgsql_big_dedupe_example contains a single list of donors rather than two lists, the sampling is done by using the same list twice. 
     I know it doesn't make much sense to do record linking between two identical data sets, but I think it's still a valid example for testing the blocking. Originally, I encountered this problem using two different data sets.

  3. Dedupe.blocker.index_fields contains the "address" field, so it is indexed (using Dedupe.blocker.index).
     However, RecordLink.blocker.index_fields does not contain any fields (although the same JSON training file is used), so no indexing is done.
     Gazetteer.blocker.index_fields is different from both, containing only the "name" field.

Having a `manage.py` file with testapp settings will help beginners like me. Is there any reason to avoid it ? 

Pardon me for being naive.

issue_title: S3 Not responding to OPTIONS request when using presigned URL from the browser
issue_body:
I'm attempting to use a pre-signed URL from within the browser to upload to my localstack S3 service. Generating the URL itself is trivial, however when attempting to POST to the resulting url (e.g `http://localstack:4572/ps.local/test?X-Amz-Content-Sha256=UNSIGNED-PAYLOAD...`), the browser makes an OPTIONS preflight request, to which localstack does not respond.

It appears as if there is no way around this even with flexible CORS rules (see below). It's not therefore possible to directly upload files from the browser to localstack S3 using presigned URL's.
```
            $s3->putBucketCors([
                'Bucket' => 'myBucket',
                'CORSConfiguration' => [
                    'CORSRules' => [
                        [
                            'AllowedOrigins' => ['*'],
                            'AllowedMethods' => ['POST', 'GET', 'PUT'],
                            'MaxAgeSeconds' => 3000,
                            'AllowedHeaders' => ['*']
                        ]
                    ]
                ]
            ]);
```

If someone can advise on whether or not I've overlooked something I'll be happy to investigate further and follow up with a PR.

Currently, there is no way to configure which version of elasticsearch localstack uses. It would be nice if we could configure it! In my case, I have plugins that I require which run on 5.x.x but not 6.x.x so I have to run elasticsearch separately to support them.

That said, this is an awesome project and I really appreciate the hard-work that's gone into it!

👉  https://opencollective.com/localstack/donate -->

issue_title: Integrating the source code in the Chinese and English projects.
issue_body:
### New Issue Checklist

- [x] I have read the [Contribution Guidelines](https://github.com/tensorlayer/tensorlayer/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [x] I searched for [existing GitHub issues](https://github.com/tensorlayer/tensorlayer/issues)

### Issue Description

Today, the Chinese and English documentations of TensorLayer are maintained in completely separated repositories, leading to poor management of source codes, duplications of CI tests, and extra maintenance and development works. Shall we considering integrating these two repositories?
What are the best way of maintaining multi-language documentations for TensorLayer.

A deeper question is that: we are having more and more documents in the types of tutorials, examples, blogs, documentations. They can be written in multiple languages. What is the best way to manage all these materials in a single repo?

issue_title: option for sr_poll to only poll when has_vip.
issue_body:
The sr_poll do_poll script executes on all participants in HA configs (whether or not has_vip is True). In the original use case, the poll script must execute on the backup node to keep the ''ls'' state uptodate on the backup nodes so that only files which haven't been transferred come over when a failover occurs.

in many other (almost all) cases, one really wants to run the poll script only on the active node.
so should add a option to sr_config.py

poll_runs_on_all_nodes (default: False)

and set it to true for the (original) NWS case, but have the default be False.

import of feature request #66 from sourceforge.

issue_title: Support for configuring on a per-subdomain basis
issue_body:
We've been using django-auth-ldap in [Zulip](https://zulipchat.com) happily for years.  As Zulip has grown, though, we've gotten requests for supporting LDAP authentication on our hosted service.

The most natural way to do this would be to have the django-auth-ldap settings configuration (things like `AUTH_LDAP_SERVER_URI`) be something that the LDAP backend looks up from e.g. database through some code that we write, rather than being something hardcoded in the Django `settings.py`.  Is there a good way to do that?

The Client.unwrap function should not pass the cubbyhole token in the request body, as Vault now expects it in the header. The workaround is to set the cubbyhole token as the client token.

This produces an error:
```
vc = hvac.Client()
unwrapped = vc.unwrap(<cubbyhole_token>)
```

Workaround:
```
vc = hvac.Client()
vc.token = <cubbyhole_token>
unwrapped = vc.unwrap()
```

issue_title: Replacing various NR routines from SBProfile Poisson.cpp
issue_body:
SBProfile code Poisson.cpp uses some NR routines (gammp, gammq, gser, gcf) with very little alteration to build a Poisson deviate from a uniform deviate.   Mike gave some references for implementing the incomplete gamma function:

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Incomplete_gamma_function
http://oai.cwi.nl/oai/asset/10080/10080A.pdf
http://www.alglib.net/specialfunctions/incompletegamma.php
http://www.gnu.org/software/gsl/manual/html_node/Incomplete-Gamma-Functions.html

He points out that "The first two could be used to roll our own.  The next two are GPL, so we could include those in our stack."
so we can optionally browse the site/docs/examples locally.
